reviews.author: Matthew Wilcox
reviews.summary: reviewer expressed confusion about the naming convention used, specifically that 'IP' is often associated with Internet Protocol rather than Instruction Pointer
reviews.raw_body:
I was very confused when I read this.  IP expands to Internet Protocol
long before it gets to Instruction Pointer in my brain.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author agrees that the patch needs to be revised based on Matthew Wilcox's feedback, but instead of revising it, he is going to follow another explanation provided by tglx.
reviews.raw_body:
Hah, fair, i was just going with tglx's [1] explanation.

https://lore.kernel.org/all/87a4s8m69c.ffs@fw13/

2026-07-04.patch_summary: This patch adds a new configuration option to make the Syscall User Dispatch feature configurable at both build and runtime. The feature is currently enabled by default for all architectures, but some users may not want it functional at runtime. A new sysctl (kernel.syscall_user_dispatch) is added to control this feature, which can be disabled independently of the CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL configuration option. Disabling the feature while a task has already enabled it will not cause it to become inactive immediately, but rather only on the next attempt to re-enable via prctl or ptrace.

reviews.author: Thomas Gleixner
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that the syscall_user_dispatch mechanism can be used to filter either within or outside of a specified range, depending on the mode passed to the prctl call, and pointed out a space/tab mismatch in the code
reviews.raw_body:
Space/TAB mismatch.

Also the mechanism allows to filter either outside an allowed range or
inside an allowed range depending on the mode which is handed to the PRCTL.

reviews.author: Thomas Gleixner
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that the include order in syscall_user_dispatch.c should be alphabetized, citing an existing issue and referencing the rule for fixing up include orders
reviews.raw_body:
It's already not ordered correctly, but the rule is that we fix up the
include order to alphabetic ordering when we touch it.

reviews.author: Randy Dunlap
reviews.summary: Reviewer Randy Dunlap simply echoed the same sentiment as the original submitter, Gregory Price, without providing any additional feedback or analysis.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that they missed the fact that the feature was already merged and agreed to use more general language in their patch.
reviews.raw_body:
hadn't noticed that feature actually got merged.

ack, will just go with the general language

2026-07-05.patch_summary: This patch adds a new configuration option to make the Syscall User Dispatch feature configurable at both build and runtime. The feature is currently enabled by default for all architectures, but some users may not want it functional at runtime. A new sysctl (kernel.syscall_user_dispatch) is added to control this feature, which can be disabled independently of the CONFIG_GENERIC_SYSCALL configuration option. Disabling the feature while a task has already enabled it will not cause it to become inactive immediately, but rather only on the next attempt to re-enable via prctl or ptrace.
